I got a hosting account, I got domain, I don't want anything else. For $5, you need to go and you need to set that thing up so when something types in my site that something comes up, I want to look just like this. And I want to say these words, and when they fill in this little box to submit, I want that email to be sent to me. $5, you get anyone in the world. You get 10,000 people to do that for $5 for you. Now you're not stuck. Russell, I'm not designing a website. You don't need to have a design a website. Just type that in an email and send it to someone. And like I said, it's a simple, simple process. Your fly capture page is up. Now after it's up, you need to get, this is where your market reaches. How do I get people to actually ask me questions? Now if you already own your own business, you already have a list that makes it real easy. I'm sure for you guys, you can put something on your blog in five minutes, you get 200 people to tell you exactly what they want. If you don't already have a business, there are a lot of ways to do it as well. One way is to go and let's say it's potato guns. When I did my potato gun one, I did two things. First thing I did is I went and there's two or three big potato gun forums. There's like spudtech.com, there's Spudapalooza, there's couple big potato gun websites. And there are people that are passionate about potato guns. And they're in these forums talking about potato guns all day long. So I just went in there and said, hey guys, great to meet you. My name is Russell. And we're creating this cool DVD about how to make a potato gun. What's your number one question about how to make potato guns? Click right here and just let me know. And I'll give you a chance to win a free course. We've got probably 20 or 30 people from those forums just to fill out this form. Boom, instant market research. Second thing we did, and I'm sure, did you guys do paper click campaigns for years? I've only personally in my entire life ever run one paper click campaign. And you're going to learn tomorrow about paper click as the most horrible, evil thing in the world. Some of you guys will love it. A lot of people are making a lot of money. I can't stand it. It's not my personality. But around one campaign, I went to Google. I set it up. I had been on Spudgun and Potato Gun. I said I'll pay $0.05 per click. And I ran it through the weekend. And every time I was typing Spudgun or Potato Gun in Google, my ad showed up. Every time they clicked on it, I paid $0.05. It cost me, I think, about $0.68 that I paid from them. People came to my site. They saw this. They fill out form click submit. I paid $0.05 for a click. And I said $68. And I got about a little over 100 questions from the paper click campaign. I turned it off on Monday. And that's the only paper click campaign I've ever personally run. We'd run it in our company, but me personally. And so I ran it. So I'm saying that because it's not that hard. I personally ran it. You guys can do it too, especially if you're niche like this. You get the research and boom, now you have the data. Now you know exactly what people want. And we're talking about this when we get the sales process. What's cool about this is if someone comes in and they see this product, they want to know how to make a potato gun. This is the DVD, actually. Stuff for auction right now. This is the last one in the office. If they come in and say, look, I should just do hair bows. So they come in, the hair bow is like, hey, I want to learn how to blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. I want to learn to do this. And you get all these people submitting questions back to you. When you get to the point where you write in the sales letter, it comes really easy. Because instead of me coming in and saying, how do I sell this person? You say, the question came in and says, I want to learn how to write barrel to chamber volume with. So my bullet point and one of my sales letters is, discover that the right barrel to chamber volume with. Question number two comes in. Hey, for work in pornography, how do I know if my husband's actually looking at pornography? Question, bullet point number one, discover how to find out if your husband's really looking at pornography. How to know if my husband is too late to actually save my marriage and really want to when I'm frustrated? Discover how it's not too late to save your marriage and blah, blah, blah. See how it works? I'm not manipulated. I'm taking what they're asking me and I'm offering up the exact same thing. Just like that my employee would say, yep, that's exactly what I do. That's what gives you the ability to do. Next step now on page number 22 is you're going to take all of this data that comes in for you. You can actually see, this is for our pornography campaign. You can see some of the questions people came back in here. Questions, will pornography ruin my relationship with my wife? That was the one from a man. How can marriage survive when pornography addiction? Oh crap, you can see the person's email address in there. Don't look closely. Don't email Jay Wendt at Lin anyway. We see how that works. Like I asked a question, got the feedback back. We took that. And the next step in this process is to create your table of contents. Can I show you these pictures right here of some of the dummy books here? We've got guitars for dummies, fundraisers for dummies, YouTube for dummies. The reason why I did that is the dummies books, the one of the best thing about them is their table of contents. Next time you go to Barnes and Noble, go to school, look at the dummy books, open up, look at the table of contents. Table of contents is amazing. The key, the whole information marketing business is the table of contents. This is where your job, the entrepreneur, ends. You need to know how to do the table of contents. To create a table of contents. So if let's say I got back 100 different questions, people ask me things. And take those 100 questions. And the reality is of those 100 questions, there's probably a lot of these are going to be very similar. So I can break it down into groups. And then you break it down. And eventually you come down with, hey, I've got probably eight or 10 core questions that people are asking me. So here's my eight to 10 core questions. You want to take it now and create your table of contents. So chapter number one is this. Chapter number two is this. Chapter number three is this. And you create your table of contents. And this is a critical thing. We're talking about product development here after our break. But with product development, if you have this table of contents, it's outlined and makes the whole rest of the process so simple. If you don't have it, you make a lot of mistakes. So creating your own table of contents. Now, I was telling Dave the story last night. When I first got into internet marketing and I got asked to speak at a seminar, I showed up. And the seminar was supposed to be seven people that were showing up at the seminar. And they were out prior at the bar and stuff at the hotel. And they were telling everybody else. And so I walked in the room. They had more people. And I walked in the room. There was like six or seven people in the room. And it was just me. And I was like, oh, crap. I was planning on seven people where we kind of hang out and talk a little bit. So I was waiting for presentations. I walked in. I was like, hey, guys, I actually forgot my notepad at paper up so I'll be right back. I walked out and I was just like, oh, crap, oh, crap. I'd never spoken to an audience in my entire life. I was just like, oh, my gosh, oh, my gosh. So I'm like, what am I going to do? I got to figure something out. So I go out at my hotel room and I get a pad of paper. I'm like, OK, what am I talking to people about? OK, I'm like, well, if I was going to teach someone from scratch, what would I do? I'm like, well, section one, I talk about this. And I wrote out an outline. Number two would be this. Number three would be this. Number four would be this. And that became my outline. Walk back down to the audience. OK, guys, thanks so much. Glad to have you here. First thing I'm going to talk about is this. I started teaching bullet point number one. Talk about 15, 20 minutes. Then talk about bullet point number two, the number three, number four. I talked through my eight-point bullet point list. Took about two hours. Someone was recording the whole thing in the back, and that was my first presentation. I was like, ah. It was also launching. I didn't know at the time, but it was launching of my business and how to make money on the internet business. Because I took that outline, recorded the DVD. That became, if anyone's interested in my free DVD, How I Turned $20 and Simple I Did to a Million Dollar Business, that free DVD was recording from that event. Again, it was free. They paid shipping hand and they got it out there. What happened, they would go through the outline. That was my first product. Took them two hours to go through the outline. But then guess what happened? I'd spend maybe 15 minutes on a topic, but people wanted a lot more information about that. So then I started creating products. We'd talk about just this topic, or just this topic, or just this topic. We started doing events like this. If you look at this event right now, the structure of this event is exactly the same structure as the outline I made on the original DVD. What I'm teaching you guys right now is the same thing I taught in the DVD, just in a lot more detail. You're getting it in a personal position. You get for free, one of them you pay a lot more money. You guys know how much you pay to be here. That's the difference behind it. But the outline is, this is going to be the fuel for your entire business. Whether it be one product, a product line, a whole bunch of other stuff, the outline is the first key. And you get that outline from taking your questions people are giving you. Take those questions, create an outline, boom. Now I have everything else we're going to talk about with product development. So creating your outline. The one thing I do with outlines and what I'm creating these titles is not just saying chapter one, SEO, chapter two, PPC, chapter three, try to turn them all into how-to statements. So how to drive more traffic to your website through the process of SEO. How to, that's going to make, again, your problem-prosthesied logings your own how-to's. OK? You guys need a break?
